mnemonics: SPLIT DECISIONS: Sum for Splitting into cases (OR), Product for Linked choices (AND)
keyPoints: The Sum Rule (Addition Principle) is used when you have mutually exclusive choices or cases.
The Product Rule (Multiplication Principle) is used when a procedure consists of a sequence of independent steps.
These rules are fundamental for counting the number of ways to perform complex tasks by breaking them down.
commonMistakes: Using the Sum Rule for non-mutually exclusive events, leading to overcounting.
Applying the Product Rule when the choices are not independent, which gives an incorrect total.
Confusing when to add (OR) versus when to multiply (AND) the number of possibilities.
